PresentationBlueprint.description: Step 3 output: narrative architecture (embedded as `architecture`) plus per-slide messaging design (`slides`). The brief lives in s02-communication-brief.json; this file embeds the architecture block so all narrative decisions travel with the slide list.

architecture.description: Deck-level narrative decisions from Phases 3a/3b (core argument, storytelling pattern, acts, hook/anchor, information hierarchy, inter-act handoffs, structural scaffold).

totalSlides.description: Total slide count. Must equal len(slides). When slideCountConstraint is 'exact', must also match that target.

slides.description: Ordered list of slides. First must be slideType=cover, last must be slideType=closing.

NarrativeArchitecture.description: Deck-level narrative decisions produced by Phases 3a (architecture) and 3b (structural scaffolding). Embedded as `architecture` inside the blueprint.

coreArgument.description: Single complete falsifiable claim that unifies the deck. Derived from s02.keyMessages[rank=1] (departures must be logged in `inferences`).

storytellingPattern.description: Chosen narrative pattern. `origin` is one of reused / composed / nested / designed.

actStructure.description: 2–4 acts. Each act has goal, emotionalArc, sections, informationDensity.

openingHook.description: Delivery-strategy summary of the cover slide — the audience-engagement framing the speaker opens with (or the one-sentence framing the cover visually embodies). Pinned to slides[0] (the cover); the cover slide's `headlineMessage` remains the on-screen title text.

closingAnchor.description: The single most memorable takeaway — the line that lands as the deck ends. Pinned to slides[-1] (the closing slide); MUST match or be the source of the closing slide's `headlineMessage`. When the closing slide is a pure thank-you/contact card, the anchor is the verbal beat delivered just before it.

informationHierarchy.description: Triage of all available content into essential / supporting / cut tiers. Every `cut` item MUST include a non-empty `reason` so downstream steps don't re-include it.

essential.description: Must appear; cutting this undermines the argument. Items may be plain strings or {item, ...} objects.

supporting.description: Strengthens the argument; include if space allows.

cut.description: Actively excluded. Each entry MUST be an object with both `item` and a non-empty `reason` — bare strings are not allowed because the reason prevents downstream steps from re-including the item.

reason.description: Why this item was cut (audience mismatch, time constraint, dilutes focus, etc.).

interActHandoffs.description: Logical connection strategy between adjacent acts (e.g. progressive-disclosure / contrast / callback / parallel).

structuralScaffold.description: Decisions on executive summary, agenda, section dividers, mid-deck recap, appendix. Must include a `rationale`.

inferences.description: Recorded departures from defaults (e.g. coreArgument deviating from rank-1 keyMessage).

CommunicationObjectives.description: What the audience should think, feel, and do after this presentation.

headlineMessage.description: The one takeaway for this slide (the 'so what'). May be null for structural slides — cover, divider, agenda, executive-summary, recap, appendix — which serve navigation/bookkeeping rather than a single argument. Every other slide MUST have a non-null value.

supportingPoints.description: Evidence, data, or examples that prove the headline. Items may be plain strings or objects with 'point' and 'sourceRef' fields (document mode).

narrativeRole.description: Function of this slide in the narrative arc. Null for divider slides (no narrative function — they are pure structural pauses) and for agenda/recap/appendix when used as bookkeeping rather than story beats.

slideType.description: Physical/structural type of the slide. Drives layout pattern selection in Step 5 and the no-headline-required exemption in validators. Distinct from narrativeRole (story function).

narrativeIntent.description: Narrative-level intent for how this slide's message should be delivered (paired with `narrativeRole`: role = function in story, narrativeIntent = mode of delivery). Must match content — e.g. don't assign `data-visualization` to a slide with no data points. Step 4c maps this to `contentForm`; Step 5d picks the final layout.

transitionIn.description: How this slide connects FROM the previous slide. Null for the first slide.

transitionOut.description: How this slide leads TO the next slide. Null for the last slide.

informationDensity.description: Expected density of information on this slide.

contentSource.description: Where the content originates. 'document'=extracted from content docs, 'generated'=LLM-authored, 'hybrid'=document facts + LLM framing.

sourceRef.description: Primary content source reference: '<filename>#<heading>' or null.

mediaAttachments.description: Media assets from s01c-content-digest.json to include on this slide. Empty or omitted when no media is used.

illustrationIntent.description: Whether this slide needs generated illustrations (decided after mediaAttachments). Omit for bookend slides (handled by structuralFamily backgrounds) and slides that don't need illustration.

MediaAttachment.description: A media asset assigned to a slide. Sourced from s01c-content-digest.json → mediaAssets.

ref.description: File path (relative to session dir) or URL of the media asset.

alt.description: Caption or alt-text for the media.

placement.description: How the media should be placed: primary-visual (dominant), supporting-visual (secondary alongside text), background (full-bleed/faded).

rationale.description: Why this media is included on this slide.

SourcedPoint.description: A supporting point with source traceability (used in document mode).

point.description: The evidence or data point text.

IllustrationIntent.description: Declares whether a slide needs generated illustration(s) and describes what they should depict.

needed.description: Whether this slide needs generated illustrations.

subject.description: Conceptual description of what the illustration(s) should depict (narrative level, not a generation prompt).

narrativePurpose.description: Why this slide needs illustration — must be a communication argument, not decorative.

placement.description: How the illustration relates to text (same enum as `mediaAttachments.placement`): primary-visual (illustration dominates), supporting-visual (text dominates, illustration alongside), background (mood/texture/full-bleed atmosphere — not informational).

imageCount.description: Number of distinct images needed on this slide.

perImageSubject.description: Per-image subject descriptions when imageCount > 1. Length must equal imageCount. Null when imageCount == 1.
